skill.name: specdd-explain
skill.description: Explains SpecDD specs, targets, behaviors, and effective spec chains without editing project files.
skill.summary: Explains active SpecDD contracts in plain language while preserving scope, authority, and constraints.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: Manual review found that the static external command findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks around file paths, skill names, and keywords. The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance findings are also false positives, and no prompt injection attempt or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged text uses Markdown backticks for file paths, a skill name, and terms such as Must and Depends on. It does not instruct Ruby or shell execution. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The surrounding context is documentation guidance, and no executable command syntax or user-controlled command construction appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inline Markdown formatting, not executable code. The file contains no scripts or command invocation logic.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged line is the YAML description field and contains no cryptographic algorithm, hashing operation, or encryption instruction. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The line is descriptive metadata, and there is no semantic evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or another weak cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is metadata text only. No cryptographic function, command, or implementation appears in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines tell the assistant not to broaden a one-spec explanation unless referenced context is needed. They do not request host, network, environment, or account discovery. Confidence: 0.9. Confidence reasoning: The instruction limits documentation scope and contains no reconnaissance command or data collection behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content narrows explanatory scope within SpecDD files. It does not direct system probing or collection of machine details.

content.value_statement: SpecDD projects can be hard to explain when several specs govern one target. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code summarize the active contract without changing files.
actual_capabilities: Explains a requested SpecDD spec, target path, behavior, or effective spec chain.
Identifies bootstrap and governing spec context before producing an explanation.
Separates direct requirements from inference, missing context, and underspecified behavior.
Highlights scope, ownership, allowed changes, boundaries, dependencies, and completion criteria.
Preserves strong contract terms such as Must, Must not, Forbids, Depends on, and write authority.
limitations: Only applies to projects that already have a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file.
Does not adopt SpecDD into a project or create new specs by itself.
Does not edit files unless the user explicitly asks for a spec or implementation change.
Requires access to the relevant SpecDD files for exact contract explanations.

use_cases.title: Explain Feature Scope
use_cases.description: Summarize the governing SpecDD contract for a feature before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Review Change Authority
use_cases.description: Clarify which local spec grants write authority before a code or spec change.

use_cases.title: Prepare Handoff Notes
use_cases.description: Turn an effective spec chain into concise notes for a teammate or reviewer.

prompt_templates.title: Explain One Spec
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single SpecDD file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-explain to explain this SpecDD spec in plain language. Focus on purpose, scope, requirements, and unclear points.

prompt_templates.title: Explain a Target Path
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a file, module, or feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-explain for this target path. Resolve the governing spec chain and explain what the active contract allows, requires, and forbids.

prompt_templates.title: Separate Requirements and Inference
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review where exact wording matters.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-explain to explain this behavior. Separate direct spec requirements from reasonable inference, missing context, and underspecified behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Effective Contract Meaning
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-explain to summarize the effective SpecDD contract for these changed files. Include ownership, boundaries, dependencies, write authority, and completion criteria.

output_examples.input: Explain the SpecDD contract for a checkout module before I edit it.
output_examples.output: A concise explanation with sections for purpose, scope, required behavior, forbidden changes, governing specs, and open questions.

output_examples.input: Tell me whether this local spec gives authority to change a shared component.
output_examples.output: A short authority review that identifies the nearest governing spec and labels any conclusion that depends on inference.

output_examples.input: Create handoff notes from the effective spec chain for these changed files.
output_examples.output: A focused summary of the applicable contract, important boundaries, dependencies, completion criteria, and unresolved points.

best_practices: Provide the target path, behavior, or spec file you want explained.
Ask for direct requirements and inferred conclusions to be labeled separately.
Use exact spec text when authorization, comparison, or review decisions matter.
anti_patterns: Using this skill to adopt SpecDD in a project that lacks a bootstrap file.
Asking it to edit implementation files when you only need a contract explanation.
Treating CLI summaries as authoritative when exact spec text is required.

faq.question: Does this skill edit specs or code?
faq.answer: No. It explains SpecDD contracts unless the user explicitly asks for a spec or implementation change.

faq.question: What projects can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is for projects that already have a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file.

faq.question: Can it explain an effective spec chain?
faq.answer: Yes. It can identify bootstrap context, governing specs, references, and the practical meaning of the chain.

faq.question: How does it handle unclear behavior?
faq.answer: It separates direct requirements from inference, missing context, and underspecified behavior.

faq.question: Can it use CLI-assisted discovery?
faq.answer: Yes, when useful and available. Exact spec text remains the source for important explanations.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found only Markdown documentation and no confirmed malicious behavior.
